sources.title: Assigning issues and pull requests to other GitHub users
positive_evidence: Documents that direct assignment depends on repository permissions
limits: Does not define a community claim-lease protocol

sources.title: Managing access to your projects
sources.source_role: Public visibility and project edit-permission boundary
positive_evidence: Separates public visibility from project edit access
limits: Project permissions do not replace repository permissions

sources.title: Configuring issue templates for your repository
limits: Submitting a form is not readiness or authorization

sources.title: About code owners
limits: Enforcement also depends on repository branch rules

sources.title: Creating discussion category forms
positive_evidence: Defines category-slug YAML forms for public Discussions
limits: Submitting a Discussion form does not admit Ready work or grant authority

sources.title: Secure use reference
positive_evidence: Documents script injection, third-party action, and credential risks

sources.title: Use GITHUB_TOKEN for authentication in workflows
limits: A read-only token does not make executed contribution code trusted

sources.title: REST API endpoints for rules
sources.source_role: Machine interface for repository ruleset inspection and maintenance
positive_evidence: Documents create, read, update, history, and branch-rule endpoints
limits: API capability does not authorize an agent or contributor to change repository settings

sources.title: Available rules for rulesets
sources.source_role: Normative rule semantics for protected branches
positive_evidence: Documents pull-request, status-check, thread-resolution, deletion, and force-push rules
limits: Required-review counts must fit the repository's actual maintainer topology

sources.title: Agentic Tools
positive_evidence: Publishes agent discovery instructions and XR device tooling for multiple coding assistants
limits: Reference precedent only; no code or permission model is inherited
